metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that measures the named outcome (e.g., post-landing block rate sample, novelty-rate check, or floor-eligibility count) so the headline goal of cycle-reduction is end-state-observable, not just delivery-observable.
findings.finding: The headline goal ('cycle-reduction') is a non-deferred outcome, but no AC measures whether review-cycle iterations, block rates, or related symptoms actually improved after the six tickets land — ACs only verify code delivery and CI health.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A 5-round post-landing sample of plan reviews shows the per-round BLOCK rate has not increased relative to the 2026-07-14 baseline, or a designated metric dashboard reflects the change.' If a live metric is unavailable, a documented manual spot-check against the six root causes suffices.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that validates the named beneficiaries' experience improved (e.g., confirm plan authors are now routed to the criteria guide on first draft, or record a before/after advisory-wall count for T5c findings).
findings.finding: The plan names the problem and its metrics in Context, but no AC provides a concrete validation mechanism (before/after comparison, operational metric target, or dogfooding step) tying delivery back to the named beneficiaries' experience.
Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering dependency in a850 (and/or 67da) stating that a850 must not start until 67da is merged to main, and document this in both children's sequencing notes.
findings.finding: 67da writes a paragraph to docs/plan-review-gate.md stating that eligibility 'currently requires a prior certified PASS signature', while a850 must overwrite that exact paragraph and its AC4 oracle asserts `grep -n "certified" docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows NO claim that eligibility requires a certified signature. These are directly contradictory end-states for the same document section, and neither child declares an ordering dependency on the other.
findings.suggested_fix: In a850's sequencing section, add: 'ALSO SEQUENCING: child 67da (docs) must land before this story; this story replaces the honest-limitation paragraph 67da adds.' In 67da, add a forward reference: 'Note: the paragraph added by AC3 will be replaced by the floor-eligibility story (a850) once that lands.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback / back-out plan (or expand-contract sequencing) for each of the three behavioral changes — dedup grouping, coaching-on-blocking attachment, and T5c overlay scoping — covering how to revert if a regression is detected post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan introduces several behavioral changes to the gate (dedup grouping of findings, coaching-note/criteria-guide deep-link attachment to blocking findings, T5c overlay scoping reduction) but states no explicit rollback or back-out plan if any of these changes cause regressions in gate behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: For each child ticket that changes gate behavior, add a 'Rollback' section: e.g., 'Revert commit X; no schema migration required' or 'Feature-flag FOO can be set to 0 to disable dedup grouping without a redeploy.' If the changes are trivially reversible via a Gerrit revert, state that explicitly so operators know the back-out path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (and any relevant ADR) will be updated as part of this epic to document the new dedup semantics, coaching-on-blocking behavior, and T5c overlay scoping — or explicitly note that a dedicated doc-update child ticket covers this.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new behavioral contracts (dedup grouping, coaching-on-blocking, T5c overlay scoping rules) and a new routing step (directing plan authors to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md), but does not mention whether docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md or any ADR needs to be updated to document these new patterns.
findings.location: Context item (5); Success Criteria 'No behavioral weakening' / child tickets
findings.suggested_fix: Add a documentation child ticket (or add a doc-update task to an existing child) that: (a) revises docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md to describe dedup grouping, coaching attachment to blocking findings, and T5c overlay scope; (b) optionally authors a short ADR for the dedup and coaching architectural decisions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the key behavioral invariants introduced by this epic: dedup-groups-but-never-drops, coaching attaches to blocking findings, T5c overlay scope restriction rationale, and floor-eligibility sequencing constraint (must follow ba33/attest.py rewrite).
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple new pipeline behavioral rules (dedup grouping, coaching-note attachment to blocking findings, T5c overlay scope restriction, author-routing logic, floor-eligibility gating) with no mention of capturing these decisions in an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design document. Future maintainers have no durable record of why these rules exist or what invariants they encode.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Context section — six child tickets described without documentation artifact
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/NNNN-plan-review-cycle-reduction-2026-07.md (or append to CLAUDE.md) with: (1) the dedup invariant and fan-out problem it solves, (2) the coaching-to-blocking-findings rule and its rationale, (3) T5c scope restriction and what 'out-of-scope' means operationally, (4) floor-eligibility dependency on CERTIFIED signatures and sequencing after ba33.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a recommended landing sequence for the five non-gated children (especially citric-alert-dogfish before petit-cordial-kudu) to the epic's Success Criteria or sequencing note.
findings.finding: The epic does not establish a recommended sequencing order among the five children that lack an explicit external dependency (all except giddying-teenytiny-mare). Children 2 (grouping) and 4 (coaching extension) both modify blocking-finding output behavior and may share touched code paths; landing them in an uncoordinated order risks merge conflicts or behavioral regression that only appears in combination.
findings.location: Success Criteria — sequencing note; child tickets citric-alert-dogfish and petit-cordial-kudu
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Success Criteria: 'Among non-gated children, land citric-alert-dogfish (grouping) before petit-cordial-kudu (coaching extension) to avoid structural mismatch between grouped finding cardinality and coaching attachment logic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] List all six child ticket identifiers (ID + one-line title) inline under Success Criteria so an executor can locate each ticket's spec without leaving the plan.
findings.finding: The plan's Success Criteria and Acceptance Criteria reference 'six child tickets' as if they are listed ('below'), but no child ticket identifiers, titles, or specs appear anywhere in the plan body. An executing agent cannot determine what the six deliverables are, which files to touch, or what each child's own acceptance criteria are.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The six child tickets below are closed with their changes landed on main'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a numbered list under Success Criteria, e.g.:
1. TICKET-NNN — Attach coaching notes to blocking findings
2. TICKET-NNN — Route plan authors to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md
… (one line per child) 
This makes 'the six child tickets below' literally true and removes the need for any out-of-band lookup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] List and briefly describe each of the six child tickets (by ID and one-line scope) in the plan body, and add or extend ACs so each ticket's key behavioral change has a verifiable criterion beyond 'ticket is closed.'
findings.finding: The plan body describes six problem drivers in Context but never names, summarizes, or links the six child tickets that are supposed to address them. All three ACs measure only process outcomes (tickets closed, Gerrit votes, CI pass) — none verify that any specific driver is actually resolved. This means every substantive deliverable is uncovered by any AC that could be verified against described work.
findings.location: Success Criteria ("six child tickets") + Acceptance Criteria AC1/AC2/AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Child Tickets' section that enumerates the six tickets with IDs and one-line descriptions (e.g., 'T-1: Add coaching notes to blocking findings — fixes driver 4'). Then add ACs such as 'Blocking findings include coaching notes and deep-links to the criteria guide' and 'Plan authors are routed to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md before LLM-tier review' to cover drivers 4 and 5 directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether driver (3) (convergence rising floor) is addressed by a child ticket in this epic that is sequenced after ba33, or is deferred alongside the Q2 items; if sequenced, add an explicit ordering constraint in Acceptance Criteria.
findings.finding: Driver (3) — the convergence rising floor never engaging — is listed in Context as a problem to remediate in this epic, but Success Criteria states 'the floor-eligibility story starts only after that epic's [ba33] plan-review commits land on main,' making it sequencing-dependent on an external epic. It is not listed alongside the Q2 exclusions, so it is ambiguous whether a child ticket addresses driver (3) immediately or whether it is also effectively deferred.
findings.location: Context driver (3) vs. Success Criteria sequencing note
findings.suggested_fix: Either add driver (3) to the explicit out-of-scope list with a note ('deferred until ba33 lands; tracked as follow-on') or add an AC such as 'The floor-eligibility child ticket is submitted to Gerrit only after ba33's final plan-review commit appears on main.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the T5c overlay scope restriction and the dedup grouping logic will be configurable (e.g., via a settings file or registry entry) or intentionally hardcoded, and document the rationale either way in the relevant child ticket or ADR.
findings.finding: The T5c overlay scope restriction rule and the dedup grouping logic are new behavioral rules expected to evolve (the plan's own context describes T5c as producing 288 advisory findings/7d with 0 blocks, implying ongoing tuning is likely) but the plan contains no indication these rules will be configurable rather than hardcoded.
findings.location: Context section — 'T5c security overlay contributes a large advisory wall (288 findings/7d, 0 blocks) including out-of-scope findings'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that medium-monogamous-dotterel's two sub-goals (author routing and gate-doc threshold correction) are tightly coupled enough to ship as one unit, or split them if either could independently unblock plan authors sooner.
findings.finding: Child ticket medium-monogamous-dotterel bundles two potentially separable outcomes: (a) routing plan-authoring guidance to authors and (b) fixing stale gate-doc thresholds. These have distinct reasons to change (workflow routing vs. documentation accuracy) and could be independently valuable if the routing change is urgent while the threshold fix requires research, or vice versa.
findings.location: Child ticket: medium-monogamous-dotterel — 'Route plan-authoring guidance to authors + fix stale gate-doc thresholds'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named enforcement mechanism for the ba33 sequencing constraint on the floor-eligibility child (e.g., a `depends_on` dep link to ba33/courtly-linear-iguana, or an explicit AC on the floor-eligibility child ticket that ba33's attest.py commits are on main before work begins).
findings.finding: The sequencing constraint between this epic's floor-eligibility child and the ba33/courtly-linear-iguana epic (which rewrites attest.py) is stated as a prose advisory but has no named enforcement mechanism — no ticket dependency link on the floor-eligibility child, no CI gate, no branch ordering guard. If ba33 lands late, partially, or is reordered, an implementer can start the floor-eligibility child on a stale attest.py, producing a merge conflict or silently broken behavior. This is a hidden ordering/atomicity assumption.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'Work is sequenced to avoid textual conflict with epic/audit-enrichment (ba33 / courtly-linear-iguana), which rewrites src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py: the floor-eligibility story starts only after that epic's plan-review commits land on main.'
findings.suggested_fix: On the floor-eligibility child ticket, add `depends_on: <ba33-ticket-id>` and an AC: 'Work does not begin until ba33/courtly-linear-iguana's attest.py commits are confirmed on main (verify via `git log main -- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`).' This makes the prose constraint machine-checkable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the plan body for why the full story/Gerrit approach is appropriate for each driver (e.g., 'driver 4 requires a behavioral change in Pass-4 output assembly, not just a prompt edit, because the coach pass only runs over advisory findings — the fix requires code changes in passes.py').
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for WHY the chosen approach (one targeted child ticket per driver, delivered through Gerrit) is preferred over a lower-friction configuration-only fix for at least two of the six drivers. Driver 4 (coaching notes attach only to advisory findings) and driver 5 (authors not routed to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md) could plausibly be addressed by prompt-file edits or registry changes without a full story/Gerrit cycle. The plan does not explain why a full story decomposition (with the overhead the epic itself identifies as a problem) is the right approach here rather than a leaner config/prompt change for those sub-problems.
findings.location: Context section (drivers 4, 5) and Success Criteria ('six child tickets below are closed with their changes landed on main through Gerrit')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the six child ticket IDs (or titles) inline in the plan body so the sequencing constraint ('floor-eligibility story starts only after ba33 lands') and the AC 'all child tickets closed' are actionable and verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan references 'The six child tickets below' as the primary delivery unit throughout Success Criteria and AC, but no child ticket identifiers, titles, or specs appear anywhere in the plan body. An executing agent consuming this plan has no schema for what each of the six remediation items implements, making the sequencing rule ('floor-eligibility story starts only after that epic's plan-review commits land on main') unactionable — it cannot be applied without knowing which child IDs implement the floor-eligibility story versus the other five.
findings.location: Success Criteria section: 'The six child tickets below are closed with their changes landed on main'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Child Tickets' section listing each ticket's ID and one-line description of its remediation scope (e.g., 'ec89 — floor-eligibility precondition check', 'cc5b — rising-floor drop math', etc.), and annotate which ticket implements the floor-eligibility story that must sequence after ba33.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the pink-elephant parenthetical in Success Criteria with a positive framing (e.g., 'coaching output is constrained to move-registry selection + bounded noun-phrase subject only') to eliminate the named-prohibition antipattern.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria section contains a pink-elephant antipattern: the negative constraint 'the reviewer never writes plan text' names the prohibited behavior inside the instruction passed to the reviewing agent. This increases salience of the prohibited action in the reviewer's active context, the canonical pink-elephant failure mode for LLM instruction design.
findings.location: Success Criteria section, third bullet: 'coaching remains move-registry-constrained (the reviewer never writes plan text)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'coaching remains move-registry-constrained (the reviewer never writes plan text)' to 'coaching output is limited to move-registry selection and a bounded noun-phrase subject (no free-form plan prose authored by the reviewer)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document in both 67da and a850 that these two children share a write target (docs/plan-review-gate.md eligibility section) and enforce landing order (67da before a850) via a Gerrit dependency or explicit sequencing note.
findings.finding: Both 67da and a850 write to the same section of docs/plan-review-gate.md (the eligibility description paragraph), creating a scope overlap and implicit shared-state risk. No coordination mechanism (Gerrit parent-child dependency, sequencing note, or conflict-avoidance instruction) is specified in either child to prevent a textual merge conflict or content regression.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a contingency criterion to the parent (or to a850) stating the monitoring mechanism for ba33's landing, the owner responsible for unblocking a850, and a fallback action if ba33 is delayed beyond a defined horizon.
findings.finding: The parent epic's success criterion SC2 sequences a850 behind ba33/courtly-linear-iguana (an external epic), but no contingency or fallback is defined if ba33 is delayed or cancelled. a850 is a hard prerequisite for the rising-floor fix (the dominant driver of the 287/382-ineligible-review problem), and the parent's AC1 ('all child tickets of this epic are closed') cannot be met if a850 is permanently blocked. The epic has no structural fallback criterion and no stated owner for monitoring ba33's progress.
findings.location: child a850-1392-55b0-4514 (Sequencing) and parent Success Criteria SC2

findings.finding: The plan silently omits the `relates_to -> 9fd5-e6ca-ca2a-48ea` link that the session log's ticket graph explicitly lists for this epic, with no stated rationale for the omission.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
